task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the calculated antipodal coordinates or region (Sumatra, Indonesia).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view shows the geography of Sumatra, Indonesia, or the search bar contains coordinates near 0.1807, 101.5322.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the 'New Placemark' or 'Properties' dialog to create a marker.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'New Placemark' or 'Google Earth - New Placemark' is visible on screen.
The 'Name' field in the placemark dialog contains the text 'Quito Antipode'.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the correct calculated antipodal coordinates into the placemark properties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Latitude field shows approx '0.1807' (North) and Longitude shows approx '101.5322' (East) in the placemark dialog.

task_completion.description: The placemark is successfully saved and visible in the 'My Places' sidebar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'My Places' panel on the left side of the interface lists an item named 'Quito Antipode'.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the Google Earth GUI directly, not via terminal or scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Google Earth application window with mouse cursors or dialogs, no terminal/console overlays performing the action.

integrity.description: Agent is using the actual Google Earth application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface includes standard Google Earth elements like the 3D globe, sidebar, toolbar, and navigation controls.

integrity.description: Agent creates the placemark manually rather than loading a pre-made KML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing into the Placemark dialog or placing a pin, rather than using 'File > Open' to load an external file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The antipodal point of Quito (0.1807°S, 78.4678°W) is Latitude 0.1807° N and Longitude 101.5322° E. This location is in Sumatra, Indonesia.
